filters.note: WHERE stmt IS NOT NULL; NULL sentinel = -1 for int16_t dict column

data_structures.aggregation_key: stmt_code * 2 + rfile_code — maps to slot [0..11], zero hash overhead
data_structures.count_distinct: bitset(86135 bits = 10767 bytes) per group per thread — 12 groups × 10767B = ~126KB per thread; 64 threads × 126KB = ~8MB total, well within 44MB LLC
data_structures.merge_strategy: sequential_merge — T×G = 64×12 = 768 << 100K threshold; bitset OR-merge across threads

fields.adsh_bitset: uint8_t[10767] — bit i set if adsh_code i seen in this group

parallelism.thread_local: each thread owns one flat array[12] of agg structs with embedded bitsets
parallelism.merge: sequential post-scan: for each thread t, for each slot k: global[k].cnt += t[k].cnt; global[k].sum_line += t[k].sum_line; global[k].count_line += t[k].count_line; global[k].adsh_bitset OR= t[k].adsh_bitset

dict_files.note: not decoded at runtime — adsh_code used as bitset index directly

output.csv_quoting: C31: double-quote ALL string columns — printf("\"%s\"", stmt_str) and printf("\"%s\"", rfile_str)
output.precision_note: line is int32_t; sum fits in int64_t; no C29 risk. AVG output as double.

correctness_checks: C15: key includes BOTH stmt_code AND rfile_code
C18: decode stmt/rfile codes via dict vectors at output time
C31: quote stmt and rfile string output columns
C2: load stmt_dict.txt and rfile_dict.txt at runtime, never hardcode codes
adsh bitset indexed by adsh_code (int32_t); must verify adsh_code fits [0, 86134]
